headline: Following Buffett: Greg Abel Cuts Berkshire Holdings Dating Back To 2011  — But The Big Names Stay Put
summary: Berkshire Hathaway changed its holdings in the first quarter, but its top 10 stocks look the same from the fourth quarter.

headline: Start Your Grills: The Oscar Mayer Wienie 500 Heats Up Year Two With Delightful Race Day Traditions and First-Ever Fan Pick
summary: CHICAGO & PITTSBURGH, May 18, 2026--After a breakout debut that quickly became a summer sensation — drawing 85,000 fans in the stands and 8 million streaming live via the Fox Sports app and @INDYCARonFOX social platforms — Oscar Mayer is firing up the grills and revving up for the second annual Wienie 500. Returning Friday, May 22, all six Wienermobiles will haul buns to the legendary Indianapolis Motor Speedway for a high-stakes race to crown the Top Dog. The race kicks off summer, proving that

headline: Inside Utz’s rise from regional potato chip brand to salty snack juggernaut
summary: The 105-year-old pretzel and chip maker is in the midst of a slow but steady national expansion as it competes with PepsiCo’s Frito-Lay and scores of smaller local brands.

headline: 1 Cash-Producing Stock to Keep an Eye On and 2 We Brush Off
summary: A company that generates cash isn’t automatically a winner. Some businesses stockpile cash but fail to reinvest wisely, limiting their ability to expand.

raw.headline: 1 Cash-Producing Stock to Keep an Eye On and 2 We Brush Off

headline: Whirlpool’s CEO Warns Consumer Spending Today Looks Like the 2008 Financial Crisis
summary: Whirlpool (NYSE:WHR) CEO Marc Bitzer is making one of the bluntest recession comparisons of this earnings cycle. According to the Morning Brew Daily podcast segment covering the company’s Q1 results, CEO Bitzer told investors: “This level of industry decline is similar to what we have observed during the global financial crisis and even higher than ...
